reasoning: The 7-year Treasury yield (DGS7) recently dropped sharply to 3.81% on Feb 13 due to weak economic data (home sales, labor market) and increased expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts. The FRED statistical trend analysis estimates a 71% probability of an increase by Feb 22, primarily driven by mean reversion from this sudden dip. Given that 3.81% is a multi-month low, a short-term upward retracement or technical bounce is highly plausible over the one-week horizon, though ongoing market sentiment about rate cuts provides downward pressure. Thus, I estimate a 62% chance of being higher than 3.81 on Feb 22. For the 1-month horizon (March 17), uncertainty increases, and while mean reversion still exerts some upward pull, the broader fundamental downward shift pushes the estimate closer to 50% (55%), effectively increasing uncertainty over the longer timeframe.
